title: Fix: get_entity should include temporal metadata (valid_at/invalid_at) on returned edges
analysis: The get_entity() method in memory_service.py (lines 796-829) returns edges with only {uuid, fact} — missing temporal metadata, edge name, entity names, and provenance. Meanwhile, the _search_graphiti() method (lines 716-763) in the same file already extracts all of these fields from the exact same Graphiti edge objects. The fix is to reuse the proven extraction pattern from _search_graphiti() in get_entity(), enriching edge dicts to include temporal (valid_at/invalid_at), name, entities, and provenance. Additionally, a valid_only parameter should be added at both the service level (memory_service.py) and MCP tool level (tools.py) to let callers filter out historically-invalidated edges. The GraphitiBackend in graphiti_client.py does NOT need changes — it already returns full Graphiti edge objects with all metadata; the problem is purely in the dict-construction step within memory_service.py. MockEdge in conftest.py already has valid_at and invalid_at fields, but needs a 'name' field added for complete test coverage of edge relationship names.

prerequisites.description: Add 'name' field to MockEdge in conftest.py (default: None) so tests can set edge relationship names. MockEdge already has valid_at, invalid_at, source_node, target_node, episodes — only 'name' is missing.

steps.description: Write failing test: test_get_entity_edges_include_temporal_metadata — create MockEdges with valid_at/invalid_at set, call service.get_entity(), assert each returned edge dict has a 'temporal' key containing {valid_at: str, invalid_at: str|None}. Also test edge with neither field returns temporal: None.

In memory_service.py get_entity(), extract valid_at and invalid_at from each edge using the same getattr+temporal-dict pattern as _search_graphiti() (lines 728-735). Add 'temporal' key to each edge dict.

Write failing test: test_get_entity_edges_include_name_entities_provenance — create MockEdges with name, source_node/target_node (MockNode), and episodes list. Assert each returned edge dict has 'name' (str), 'entities' (list of node names), and 'provenance' (list of episode ID strings).

In memory_service.py get_entity(), extract name (getattr with default None), entities (from source_node.name/target_node.name), and provenance (from episodes list) using the same pattern as _search_graphiti() (lines 737-748). Add all three keys to each edge dict.

Write failing test: test_get_entity_valid_only_filters_invalidated_edges — create 3 MockEdges: one with invalid_at=None (valid), one with invalid_at set (invalidated), one with neither. Call get_entity(valid_only=True), assert only edges without non-null invalid_at are returned. Also test default (valid_only=False) returns all edges.
Add valid_only: bool = False parameter to memory_service.get_entity(). When True, skip edges where getattr(edge, 'invalid_at', None) is not None. Apply filter before building edge_data list.

Write failing test: test_get_entity_tool_accepts_valid_only — verify the MCP tool handler in tools.py passes valid_only to memory_service.get_entity(). Mock memory_service.get_entity, call the tool function with valid_only=True, assert the mock was called with valid_only=True.
Add valid_only: bool = False parameter to the get_entity MCP tool in tools.py. Update docstring to document the parameter. Pass it through to memory_service.get_entity(valid_only=valid_only).

design_decisions.decision: Enrich edge dicts in memory_service.py rather than changing GraphitiBackend
design_decisions.rationale: GraphitiBackend.search() already returns full Graphiti edge objects with all temporal metadata. The data loss happens in memory_service.py's dict-construction loop (lines 822-827) which only extracts uuid and fact. Fixing at this layer is minimal, targeted, and consistent with _search_graphiti() which solves the same problem for the search() path.

design_decisions.decision: Use the same temporal dict structure as _search_graphiti() — {valid_at: str|None, invalid_at: str|None} nested under a 'temporal' key
design_decisions.rationale: Consistency with search() results. Agents already parse this structure from search results (see MemoryResult.temporal field). Using the same format means consuming code doesn't need different parsing paths for search vs get_entity.

design_decisions.decision: Add name, entities, and provenance to edges alongside temporal metadata
design_decisions.rationale: While the task specifically calls out temporal metadata, _search_graphiti() also extracts name, entities, and provenance — and get_entity lacks all of them. Including these makes get_entity results complete and avoids a follow-up task. The extraction code is already proven in _search_graphiti().
Backward compatibility — existing callers expect all edges returned. Agents that need only valid edges opt in explicitly. This also lets reconciliation agents see the full history when needed.

design_decisions.decision: Filter valid_only in memory_service (application layer) not GraphitiBackend (query layer)
design_decisions.rationale: The Graphiti search() API doesn't support filtering by invalid_at natively. Adding a Cypher query variant in GraphitiBackend would be a larger change. Since get_entity returns at most ~10 edges (num_results=10), post-fetch filtering is negligible cost and keeps the fix contained.

The task description mentions graphiti_client.py but inspection shows there is no get_entity wrapper there. The backend's search() and search_nodes() return full objects. All changes are in memory_service.py and tools.py.

reuse.how: Copy the getattr-based extraction of valid_at/invalid_at and temporal dict construction directly into get_entity()'s edge loop
Copy the source_node/target_node name extraction into get_entity()'s edge loop
Copy the episodes-to-provenance-list conversion into get_entity()'s edge loop
Use existing MockEdge (already has valid_at, invalid_at, source_node, target_node, episodes) and MockNode (has name) in new get_entity tests — only need to add 'name' field to MockEdge

reuse.what: Service test fixture with mocked backends
reuse.how: Reuse the existing 'service' fixture which provides MemoryService with mocked graphiti.search, graphiti.search_nodes, etc. Set return values with MockEdge/MockNode instances.
